title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server Suspicious Tool-Invocation, Authentication-Anomaly, and Prompt-Driven-Misuse Detection
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.4.1 through UC-13.4.6 LLM provider and self-hosted inference analytics, UC-13.4.15 MLTK drift statistics, generic endpoint detection without MCP tool semantics, or data-loss prevention on unstructured egress unrelated to tool calls.
evidence: Saved search security_uc_13_4_7_mcp_tool_invocation_threat on a ten-minute schedule; Enterprise Security incident review with drilldowns to mcp_audit and otel traces; weekly CSV exports of session_id and mcp_server_id archived to a restricted security_evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab MCP server, emit mcp_audit rows where agent_id issues more than two hundred tool calls in one minute against high tool_risk_class without allowlist tier internal_batch, plus a tenant_id mismatch versus mcp_authorized_clients.csv; confirm security_uc_13_4_7_mcp_tool_invocation_threat returns critical or high with non-null detection_reason within two ten-minute windows.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After removing test traffic, when agents match allowed_tenant and client_tier internal_batch for dense read-only workloads and bearer hashes stabilize, confirm severities fall to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only.

dataSources: index=mcp_audit MCP tool invocation audit with mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name auth_method tenant_id session_id; index=mcp_logs MCP listener health stdio and HTTP transport errors; index=otel_traces sourcetype=otel:span agent to MCP to tool spans; index=mcp_security IDS and UEBA-style notables; KV lookups mcp_tool_inventory.csv and mcp_authorized_clients.csv; accelerated CIM Authentication and Change for tstats enrichment
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector, Splunk MCP Server or Anthropic-compatible MCP implementations, HTTP Event Collector
spl:
`comment("UC-13.4.7 MCP server tool-invocation security: multisearch mcp_audit mcp_logs otel:span mcp_security with inventory joins, per-agent streamstats, tstats Authentication and Change enrichment. Tunables earliest=-4h@h tpm_warn=180 tpm_page=480")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=mcp_audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="mcp_audit"
      | eval mcp_server_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mcp_server_id,mcp_instance,server_guid,"unscoped_mcp"))))
      | eval client_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_id,oauth_client_id,caller_id,""))))
      | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(agent_id,assistant_id,automation_principal,client_id,"unknown_agent"))))
      | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,invoked_tool,method,""))))
      | eval tool_category=toString(coalesce(tool_category,""))
      | eval tool_risk_class=toString(coalesce(tool_risk_class,""))
      | eval auth_method=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_method,scheme,"unlabeled"))))
      | eval bearer_token_hash=toString(coalesce(bearer_token_hash,token_fp,jti_hash,""))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,org_id,account_id,""))))
      | eval prompt_input_hash=toString(coalesce(prompt_input_hash,user_turn_digest,""))
      | eval tool_arg_hash=toString(coalesce(tool_arg_hash,arguments_digest,arg_fp,""))
      | eval tool_response_size=tonumber(coalesce(tool_response_size,response_bytes,payload_out,0))
      | eval transport=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(transport,io_channel,"stdio"))))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id,conn_id,correlation_id,""))
      | eval error_code=toString(coalesce(error_code,result_code,http_status,""))
      | fields _time lane mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class auth_method bearer_token_hash tenant_id prompt_input_hash tool_arg_hash tool_response_size transport session_id error_code ]
    [ search index=mcp_logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="mcp_logs"
      | eval mcp_server_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mcp_server_id,listener_id,host_key,"unscoped_mcp"))))
      | eval client_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_id,peer_id,""))))
      | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(agent_id,upstream_agent,client_id,"unknown_agent"))))
      | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,"transport_event"))))
      | eval tool_category="control_plane"
      | eval tool_risk_class="low"
      | eval auth_method=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_method,"unlabeled"))))
      | eval bearer_token_hash=toString(coalesce(bearer_token_hash,""))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,""))))
      | eval prompt_input_hash=""
      | eval tool_arg_hash=toString(coalesce(tool_arg_hash,""))
      | eval tool_response_size=tonumber(coalesce(tool_response_size,frame_bytes,0))
      | eval transport=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(transport,wire,"stdio"))))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id,socket_session,""))
      | eval error_code=toString(coalesce(error_code,disconnect_reason,""))
      | fields _time lane mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class auth_method bearer_token_hash tenant_id prompt_input_hash tool_arg_hash tool_response_size transport session_id error_code ]
    [ search index=otel_traces sourcetype=otel:span earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="otel_span"
      | eval mcp_server_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mcp_server_id,resource_service_name,service_name,"unscoped_mcp"))))
      | eval client_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_id,net_peer_ip,""))))
      | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(agent_id,trace_agent_id,client_id,"unknown_agent"))))
      | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,span_name,operation,"otel_span"))))
      | eval tool_category="trace"
      | eval tool_risk_class="low"
      | eval auth_method="otel"
      | eval bearer_token_hash=""
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,resource_tenant,""))))
      | eval prompt_input_hash=toString(coalesce(prompt_input_hash,""))
      | eval tool_arg_hash=toString(coalesce(tool_arg_hash,span_id,""))
      | eval tool_response_size=tonumber(coalesce(tool_response_size,span_bytes,0))
      | eval transport=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(transport,exporter_protocol,"http"))))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id,trace_id,""))
      | eval error_code=toString(coalesce(error_code,status_code,""))
      | fields _time lane mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class auth_method bearer_token_hash tenant_id prompt_input_hash tool_arg_hash tool_response_size transport session_id error_code ]
    [ search index=mcp_security earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="mcp_security"
      | eval mcp_server_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mcp_server_id,dest_host,"unscoped_mcp"))))
      | eval client_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_id,src_identity,""))))
      | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(agent_id,risk_object,client_id,"unknown_agent"))))
      | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,signature_id,"notable_tool"))))
      | eval tool_category="ueba"
      | eval tool_risk_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_risk_class,risk_band,"high"))))
      | eval auth_method=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_method,"notable"))))
      | eval bearer_token_hash=toString(coalesce(bearer_token_hash,""))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,""))))
      | eval prompt_input_hash=toString(coalesce(prompt_input_hash,""))
      | eval tool_arg_hash=toString(coalesce(tool_arg_hash,anomaly_vector_fp,""))
      | eval tool_response_size=0
      | eval transport=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(transport,"http"))))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id,episode_id,""))
      | eval error_code=toString(coalesce(error_code,severity,""))
      | fields _time lane mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class auth_method bearer_token_hash tenant_id prompt_input_hash tool_arg_hash tool_response_size transport session_id error_code ]
| eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,""))))
| join type=left max=0 tool_name
    [| inputlookup mcp_tool_inventory.csv
     | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(tool_name)))
     | fields tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class ]
| eval tool_category=coalesce(nullif(tool_category,""),"unclassified")
| eval tool_risk_class=lower(coalesce(nullif(tool_risk_class,""),"medium"))
| join type=left max=0 agent_id
    [| inputlookup mcp_authorized_clients.csv
     | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(agent_id)))
     | fields agent_id allowed_tenant allowed_transport client_tier ]
| eval tenant_mismatch=if(isnotnull(allowed_tenant) AND len(tostring(allowed_tenant))>1 AND len(tostring(tenant_id))>1 AND tenant_id!=allowed_tenant,1,0)
| eval transport_violation=if(isnotnull(allowed_transport) AND len(tostring(allowed_transport))>1 AND !match(transport,tostring(allowed_transport)),1,0)
| eval arg_probe=lower(toString(tool_arg_hash))
| eval prompt_injection_like=if(match(arg_probe,"(?i)ignore|jailbreak|base64|%00|\\\\x[0-9a-f]{2}") OR match(lower(toString(prompt_input_hash)),"(?i)dan\\\\b|developer_mode"),1,0)
| eval unauth_ok=if(match(auth_method,"none|anonymous|unlabeled") AND match(error_code,"(?i)ok|0|200|success") AND match(tool_risk_class,"high|critical"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1m as minute_bucket
| sort 0 agent_id mcp_server_id + _time
| streamstats window=10 current=t global=f count AS win_n dc(bearer_token_hash) AS win_token_dc sum(eval(if(match(tool_name,"^read_|^list_|^get_"),1,0))) AS win_reads sum(eval(if(match(tool_name,"^(delete_|exec_|run_|shell_)"),1,0))) AS win_destruct BY agent_id mcp_server_id
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f last(tool_risk_class) AS prev_risk BY agent_id
| eval sensitive_fanout=if(win_destruct>=1 AND win_reads==0 AND win_n>=4,1,0)
| eval rapid_escalation=if(match(tool_risk_class,"high|critical") AND match(prev_risk,"low|medium|unclassified") AND win_n<=6,1,0)
| eventstats avg(win_n) AS avg_win perc90(win_n) AS p90_win BY agent_id
| eval tpm_burst=if(win_n>=180 OR (isnotnull(p90_win) AND p90_win>0 AND win_n>10*p90_win),1,0)
| eval auth_churn=if(win_token_dc>=3 AND win_n>=5,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 client_id
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Authentication.user) AS auth_user latest(Authentication.action) AS auth_action FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.src
     | rename Authentication.src AS client_id ]
| join type=left max=0 mcp_server_id
    [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS change_hits FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-168h@h latest=now BY Change.dest
     | rename Change.dest AS mcp_server_id ]
| eval severity=case(
    tenant_mismatch==1 OR prompt_injection_like==1, "critical",
    tpm_burst==1 AND coalesce(client_tier,"")!="internal_batch", "critical",
    sensitive_fanout==1 OR rapid_escalation==1, "high",
    auth_churn==1 OR transport_violation==1, "high",
    unauth_ok==1, "high",
    match(error_code,"(?i)payload_too_large|timeout|reset|econn") AND win_n>20, "medium",
    true(), "info")
| eval detection_reason=case(
    tenant_mismatch==1,"cross_tenant_or_scope_mismatch",
    prompt_injection_like==1,"prompt_injection_or_encoded_args",
    tpm_burst==1,"tool_invocation_rate_anomaly",
    sensitive_fanout==1,"destructive_without_recon_pattern",
    rapid_escalation==1,"low_to_high_risk_escalation_seconds",
    auth_churn==1,"bearer_rotation_or_token_churn",
    transport_violation==1,"client_transport_policy_violation",
    unauth_ok==1,"unauthenticated_high_risk_success",
    true(),"mcp_security_fusion_context")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical","isolate_agent_session_rotate_mcp_credentials_review_tool_allowlist_open_soc_ticket",
    severity=="high","force_reauth_review_authorization_server_logs_quarantine_mcp_server_if_needed",
    severity=="medium","tune_rate_limits_review_otel_and_stdio_health_check_proxy_timeouts",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_refresh_lookups")
| stats max(_time) AS _time values(lane) AS lanes max(tpm_burst) AS tpm_burst max(tenant_mismatch) AS tenant_mismatch max(prompt_injection_like) AS prompt_injection_like max(sensitive_fanout) AS sensitive_fanout max(rapid_escalation) AS rapid_escalation max(auth_churn) AS auth_churn max(transport_violation) AS transport_violation max(unauth_ok) AS unauth_ok max(win_n) AS peak_window_tools max(change_hits) AS change_hits values(auth_user) AS auth_user first(client_tier) AS client_tier max(eval(if(severity=="critical",4,if(severity=="high",3,if(severity=="medium",2,1))))) AS sev_rank BY minute_bucket mcp_server_id agent_id client_id session_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class transport tenant_id
| eval severity=case(sev_rank>=4,"critical",sev_rank==3,"high",sev_rank==2,"medium",true(),"info")
| eval detection_reason=case(tenant_mismatch>=1,"cross_tenant_or_scope_mismatch", prompt_injection_like>=1,"prompt_injection_or_encoded_args", tpm_burst>=1,"tool_invocation_rate_anomaly", sensitive_fanout>=1,"destructive_without_recon_pattern", rapid_escalation>=1,"low_to_high_risk_escalation_seconds", auth_churn>=1,"bearer_rotation_or_token_churn", transport_violation>=1,"client_transport_policy_violation", unauth_ok>=1,"unauthenticated_high_risk_success", true(),"mcp_security_fusion_context")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","isolate_agent_session_rotate_mcp_credentials_review_tool_allowlist_open_soc_ticket", severity=="high","force_reauth_review_authorization_server_logs_quarantine_mcp_server_if_needed", severity=="medium","tune_rate_limits_review_otel_and_stdio_health_check_proxy_timeouts", true(),"continue_monitoring_refresh_lookups")
| table minute_bucket mcp_server_id agent_id client_id session_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class transport tenant_id severity detection_reason tpm_burst tenant_mismatch prompt_injection_like sensitive_fanout auth_churn peak_window_tools auth_user change_hits recommended_action lanes client_tier
description: Detects Model Context Protocol server abuse and unsafe agent behavior through fused telemetry: extreme per-agent tool invocation rates indicative of runaway automation or jailbreak loops, authentication anomalies such as bearer churn and unauthenticated success on high-risk tools, destructive tool fan-out without prior read reconnaissance, rapid escalation from low-risk to high-risk tools, cross-tenant scope mismatches, transport and payload health anomalies, and prompt-injection-like patterns in hashed arguments correlated with OpenTelemetry spans and security notables.
value: Security and platform leaders gain a tamper-resistant ledger that separates honest high-volume automation from tool-sprawl attacks and broker misconfigurations before agents exfiltrate data or mutate production systems, while analysts inherit inventory-grounded risk labels, identity context from Authentication summaries, and infrastructure change counts that accelerate Enterprise Security investigations without waiting on vendor-only AI consoles.
implementation: Land MCP audit, listener health, otel:span, and security notable events into the named indexes with hashed arguments; maintain mcp_tool_inventory.csv and mcp_authorized_clients.csv; map CIM Authentication and Change where applicable; save security_uc_13_4_7_mcp_tool_invocation_threat every ten minutes with earliest=-4h@h; route critical and high to AI platform security; archive weekly evidence with session_id and mcp_server_id facets.
visualization: Severity-colored table by mcp_server_id and agent_id with drilldowns to mcp_audit JSON, mcp_logs transport errors, otel traces, Enterprise Security episodes, lookup-enriched tool_risk_class, and tpm_burst timelines.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Authentication.user) AS auth_user count FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.src
| join type=left max=0 Authentication.src [| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-168h@h latest=now BY Change.dest ]
| table Authentication.src auth_user count Change.dest
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Authentication summaries for OAuth, workload identity, and VPN sources keyed on Authentication.src matching MCP client_id, and Change summaries for MCP host configuration objects mapped to Change.dest equal to mcp_server_id; retain thirty days for insider investigations.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Artificial intelligence and security
MITRE ATT&CK — T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter

knownFalsePositives: Legitimate batch indexing agents can emit hundreds of read_file or list_directory calls per minute during repository scans; annotate client_tier internal_batch and raise tpm thresholds rather than paging on volume alone. Scheduled IT automation that fans out delete or exec tools after change windows may match sensitive_fanout until you require preceding read phases in the same session or stamp approved_change_id on events. Large data-import sessions can trigger payload_limit_exceeded and timeout error_code clusters that mirror attacks; correlate with known migration tickets and network maintenance. AI-assisted code review tools may issue dense read bursts across many paths; allowlist repository agent identities in mcp_authorized_clients.csv. Planned data migrations with bulk delete operations should carry change metadata so rapid_escalation heuristics defer to maintenance calendars. Developer laptops running local stdio MCP servers can show disconnect noise from sleep and resume; filter by production mcp_server_id classes. Penetration tests and purple-team exercises that deliberately inject jailbreak strings should route to lab indexes. Stale inventory CSV rows after rapid CI rollouts can mislabel tool_risk_class until pipelines refresh; treat inventory drift as an operational gap not a silent mute. Duplicate log streams during blue-green cutovers can double-count streamstats windows until dedupe keys land. Brief Authentication tstats lag can empty auth_user enrichment; repair acceleration before muting the detection.

premiumApps.name: Splunk Enterprise Security
premiumApps.note: notable fusion, risk-based alerting, and Authentication or Change datamodel tstats joins

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with AI platform security, identity engineers who operate OAuth and workload identity for agent runtimes, MCP server maintainers shipping Anthropic-style tool routers and Splunk MCP Server deployments, observability engineers forwarding OpenTelemetry traces from agent hosts, and Splunk Enterprise Security analysts who consume notables alongside this correlation search. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.4.1 managed-provider latency analytics, not UC-13.4.2 token cost and FinOps pacing, not UC-13.4.3 accelerator duty-cycle economics, not UC-13.4.4 model artifact versioning, not UC-13.4.5 AI gateway throttle fairness, not UC-13.4.6 self-hosted Ollama and vLLM endpoint abuse focused on raw inference HTTP, and not UC-13.4.15 classical ML drift inside Splunk MLTK. The narrative here is Model Context Protocol server planes: structured tool catalogs exposed to agents, stdio and HTTP transports, session-scoped authorization, per-tool risk classes, and cross-tenant data boundaries that fail closed when telemetry is complete.

Treat bearer_token_hash, prompt_input_hash, tool_arg_hash, and tool_response_size as sensitive even when hashed. Raw prompts, raw JSON-RPC bodies, and live secrets must not land in Splunk without legal review and field filtering. RBAC for collectors that read MCP server logs, reverse-proxy access logs, and OpenTelemetry exporters must use short-lived credentials tied to automation roles, not administrator keys on laptops. Network documentation should show which agents may open stdio pipes versus HTTP streaming to MCP listeners, and which service meshes terminate mutual TLS.

Index design: designate index=mcp_audit for append-only tool invocation records emitted by MCP server middleware with fields such as mcp_server_id, client_id, agent_id, tool_name, auth_method, bearer_token_hash, tenant_id, prompt_input_hash, tool_arg_hash, tool_response_size, transport, session_id, and error_code. Designate index=mcp_logs for server health, disconnect storms, payload limit events, and authentication outcomes at the transport edge. Designate index=otel_traces with sourcetype=otel:span for distributed traces that stitch agent runtimes to MCP spans and downstream tool executions. Designate index=mcp_security for IDS-style signatures, UEBA notables, and fusion rows produced by Splunk Enterprise Security correlation searches referencing MCP assets.

Retention should cover at least fourteen days hot for incident replay of rapid tool bursts, thirty days for insider investigations spanning credential rotation, and cold storage aligned to regulated AI governance policy. Stamp change_ticket_id on maintenance events that alter MCP tool manifests or risk classifications so Change datamodel joins explain benign spikes.

Lookup governance: maintain mcp_tool_inventory.csv with columns tool_name, tool_category, and tool_risk_class refreshed from CI whenever servers register new tools. Maintain mcp_authorized_clients.csv with agent_id, allowed_tenant, allowed_transport, and client_tier so SPL can flag cross-tenant access and transport policy drift without hard-coding principals.

Field normalization: coalesce ladders must absorb exporter renames across Python, Node, and Go MCP servers. tool_risk_class should use a small vocabulary such as low, medium, high, and critical aligned to your governance board. transport must distinguish stdio and http consistently. session_id should correlate a single agent conversation window even when bearer_token_hash rotates under refresh-token semantics.

Risk briefing: legitimate batch automation can emit hundreds of read_file style calls during repository indexing; require client_tier annotations and statistical baselines before paging executives. Approved red-team exercises will mimic jailbreak strings inside tool arguments; route those events to lab indexes or tag them with exercise_id. Health storms from payload_limit_exceeded may cluster during model-assisted migrations; pair with Change summaries before treating as attack.

Differentiation recap: MCP server tool invocation security, authentication anomaly, prompt-driven misuse, transport health, and cross-tenant scope checks fused across audit, logs, traces, and security indexes—not generic LLM latency dashboards, not pure token accounting, not GPU thermals, not model-card drift statistics, not inference-runtime abuse patterns that never touch MCP tool catalogs.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up four complementary lanes that multisearch fans wide, plus CSV governance artifacts and optional CIM-aligned enrichment for Authentication and Change summaries.

Lane A, MCP audit JSON: emit one event per tool call with deterministic tool_name strings, allow or deny outcomes, latency when available, and hashed arguments. Include mcp_server_id cluster or listener identifier, client_id from OAuth or workload identity, agent_id that names the automation principal, tenant_id for SaaS boundaries, and session_id for conversation correlation. Map auth_method values such as bearer, mtls, and none consistently.

Lane B, MCP access and health logs: capture stdio disconnect bursts, HTTP streaming timeouts, TLS handshake failures, rate-limit responses, and payload size violations with transport and error_code fields. Forward load balancer lines in front of HTTP MCP listeners with scrubbed query strings.

Lane C, OpenTelemetry spans: configure the Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector or equivalent to export spans where span names or attributes reference MCP tool execution, parent context from agent processes, and peer identifiers. Land in index=otel_traces with sourcetype=otel:span. Preserve trace_id and span_id for drilldowns.

Lane D, security analytics: land Enterprise Security notables, risk-based alerting outputs, or custom correlation searches that reference MCP hosts as dest or risk_object inside index=mcp_security. Include signature identifiers compatible with tool_risk_class vocabulary when possible.

Lookup maintenance: publish mcp_tool_inventory.csv from the same pipeline that registers tools with MCP servers so delete_file and exec_command never appear without current risk labels. Publish mcp_authorized_clients.csv from identity governance jobs; include client_tier values such as internal_batch, interactive_copilot, and partner_integration for false-positive tuning.

CIM posture: map high-value authentication logs into the Authentication datamodel so tstats arms resolve auth_user by client_id. Map infrastructure change events that touch MCP server binaries, configuration files, and listener ports into the Change datamodel keyed on Change.dest equal to mcp_server_id when feasible.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE safely for wide JSON-RPC, honor TIMESTAMP_FIELDS from server clocks, and SEDCMD or transforms to remove live bearer tokens while preserving bearer_token_hash. For otel:span, FIELDALIAS resource.attributes.service.name to mcp_server_id when that convention holds.

Pre-save validation: confirm each index returns rows in the last hour during steady traffic, that a synthetic burst of benign read_file calls appears only in staging, and that lookup joins resolve tool_risk_class without null spikes for catalog tools.

Dashboards: host a severity-colored matrix by mcp_server_id and agent_id with drilldowns to raw audit JSON, otel traces, transport errors, and Enterprise Security episodes.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as security_uc_13_4_7_mcp_tool_invocation_threat with a five- to fifteen-minute schedule during production, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is critical or high after deduplication keyed on agent_id, mcp_server_id, and detection_reason. Throttle duplicate tuples for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action, detection_reason, and session_id in pager bodies so responders pause agents, rotate credentials, and open Enterprise Security incidents without guesswork.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch merges mcp_audit, mcp_logs, otel:span, and mcp_security so a silent single lane cannot hide cross-plane abuse when attackers mix health noise with tool bursts. coalesce() ladders absorb field renames. join to mcp_tool_inventory.csv supplies tool_category and authoritative tool_risk_class. join to mcp_authorized_clients.csv supplies allowed_tenant and allowed_transport for cross-tenant and transport policy checks. streamstats windows approximate per-agent invocation density, read versus destructive ratios, token hash diversity, and rapid escalation from medium to high risk classes inside short horizons. eventstats adds rolling context such as average and ninetieth percentile window counts for tpm_burst heuristics. Subsearch tstats on Authentication enriches client_id with auth_user for analyst triage. Subsearch tstats on Change counts recent infrastructure touches per mcp_server_id to separate patch storms from malicious behavior. case() maps combined signals into severity tiers and recommended_action tokens aligned to SOC runbooks.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.4.7 MCP server tool-invocation security: multisearch mcp_audit mcp_logs otel:span mcp_security with inventory joins, per-agent streamstats, tstats Authentication and Change enrichment. Tunables earliest=-4h@h tpm_warn=180 tpm_page=480")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=mcp_audit earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="mcp_audit"
      | eval mcp_server_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mcp_server_id,mcp_instance,server_guid,"unscoped_mcp"))))
      | eval client_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_id,oauth_client_id,caller_id,""))))
      | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(agent_id,assistant_id,automation_principal,client_id,"unknown_agent"))))
      | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,invoked_tool,method,""))))
      | eval tool_category=toString(coalesce(tool_category,""))
      | eval tool_risk_class=toString(coalesce(tool_risk_class,""))
      | eval auth_method=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_method,scheme,"unlabeled"))))
      | eval bearer_token_hash=toString(coalesce(bearer_token_hash,token_fp,jti_hash,""))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,org_id,account_id,""))))
      | eval prompt_input_hash=toString(coalesce(prompt_input_hash,user_turn_digest,""))
      | eval tool_arg_hash=toString(coalesce(tool_arg_hash,arguments_digest,arg_fp,""))
      | eval tool_response_size=tonumber(coalesce(tool_response_size,response_bytes,payload_out,0))
      | eval transport=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(transport,io_channel,"stdio"))))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id,conn_id,correlation_id,""))
      | eval error_code=toString(coalesce(error_code,result_code,http_status,""))
      | fields _time lane mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class auth_method bearer_token_hash tenant_id prompt_input_hash tool_arg_hash tool_response_size transport session_id error_code ]
    [ search index=mcp_logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="mcp_logs"
      | eval mcp_server_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mcp_server_id,listener_id,host_key,"unscoped_mcp"))))
      | eval client_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_id,peer_id,""))))
      | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(agent_id,upstream_agent,client_id,"unknown_agent"))))
      | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,"transport_event"))))
      | eval tool_category="control_plane"
      | eval tool_risk_class="low"
      | eval auth_method=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_method,"unlabeled"))))
      | eval bearer_token_hash=toString(coalesce(bearer_token_hash,""))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,""))))
      | eval prompt_input_hash=""
      | eval tool_arg_hash=toString(coalesce(tool_arg_hash,""))
      | eval tool_response_size=tonumber(coalesce(tool_response_size,frame_bytes,0))
      | eval transport=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(transport,wire,"stdio"))))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id,socket_session,""))
      | eval error_code=toString(coalesce(error_code,disconnect_reason,""))
      | fields _time lane mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class auth_method bearer_token_hash tenant_id prompt_input_hash tool_arg_hash tool_response_size transport session_id error_code ]
    [ search index=otel_traces sourcetype=otel:span earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="otel_span"
      | eval mcp_server_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mcp_server_id,resource_service_name,service_name,"unscoped_mcp"))))
      | eval client_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_id,net_peer_ip,""))))
      | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(agent_id,trace_agent_id,client_id,"unknown_agent"))))
      | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,span_name,operation,"otel_span"))))
      | eval tool_category="trace"
      | eval tool_risk_class="low"
      | eval auth_method="otel"
      | eval bearer_token_hash=""
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,resource_tenant,""))))
      | eval prompt_input_hash=toString(coalesce(prompt_input_hash,""))
      | eval tool_arg_hash=toString(coalesce(tool_arg_hash,span_id,""))
      | eval tool_response_size=tonumber(coalesce(tool_response_size,span_bytes,0))
      | eval transport=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(transport,exporter_protocol,"http"))))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id,trace_id,""))
      | eval error_code=toString(coalesce(error_code,status_code,""))
      | fields _time lane mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class auth_method bearer_token_hash tenant_id prompt_input_hash tool_arg_hash tool_response_size transport session_id error_code ]
    [ search index=mcp_security earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lane="mcp_security"
      | eval mcp_server_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mcp_server_id,dest_host,"unscoped_mcp"))))
      | eval client_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(client_id,src_identity,""))))
      | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(agent_id,risk_object,client_id,"unknown_agent"))))
      | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,signature_id,"notable_tool"))))
      | eval tool_category="ueba"
      | eval tool_risk_class=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_risk_class,risk_band,"high"))))
      | eval auth_method=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(auth_method,"notable"))))
      | eval bearer_token_hash=toString(coalesce(bearer_token_hash,""))
      | eval tenant_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tenant_id,""))))
      | eval prompt_input_hash=toString(coalesce(prompt_input_hash,""))
      | eval tool_arg_hash=toString(coalesce(tool_arg_hash,anomaly_vector_fp,""))
      | eval tool_response_size=0
      | eval transport=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(transport,"http"))))
      | eval session_id=toString(coalesce(session_id,episode_id,""))
      | eval error_code=toString(coalesce(error_code,severity,""))
      | fields _time lane mcp_server_id client_id agent_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class auth_method bearer_token_hash tenant_id prompt_input_hash tool_arg_hash tool_response_size transport session_id error_code ]
| eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(tool_name,""))))
| join type=left max=0 tool_name
    [| inputlookup mcp_tool_inventory.csv
     | eval tool_name=lower(trim(toString(tool_name)))
     | fields tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class ]
| eval tool_category=coalesce(nullif(tool_category,""),"unclassified")
| eval tool_risk_class=lower(coalesce(nullif(tool_risk_class,""),"medium"))
| join type=left max=0 agent_id
    [| inputlookup mcp_authorized_clients.csv
     | eval agent_id=lower(trim(toString(agent_id)))
     | fields agent_id allowed_tenant allowed_transport client_tier ]
| eval tenant_mismatch=if(isnotnull(allowed_tenant) AND len(tostring(allowed_tenant))>1 AND len(tostring(tenant_id))>1 AND tenant_id!=allowed_tenant,1,0)
| eval transport_violation=if(isnotnull(allowed_transport) AND len(tostring(allowed_transport))>1 AND !match(transport,tostring(allowed_transport)),1,0)
| eval arg_probe=lower(toString(tool_arg_hash))
| eval prompt_injection_like=if(match(arg_probe,"(?i)ignore|jailbreak|base64|%00|\\\\x[0-9a-f]{2}") OR match(lower(toString(prompt_input_hash)),"(?i)dan\\\\b|developer_mode"),1,0)
| eval unauth_ok=if(match(auth_method,"none|anonymous|unlabeled") AND match(error_code,"(?i)ok|0|200|success") AND match(tool_risk_class,"high|critical"),1,0)
| bin _time span=1m as minute_bucket
| sort 0 agent_id mcp_server_id + _time
| streamstats window=10 current=t global=f count AS win_n dc(bearer_token_hash) AS win_token_dc sum(eval(if(match(tool_name,"^read_|^list_|^get_"),1,0))) AS win_reads sum(eval(if(match(tool_name,"^(delete_|exec_|run_|shell_)"),1,0))) AS win_destruct BY agent_id mcp_server_id
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f last(tool_risk_class) AS prev_risk BY agent_id
| eval sensitive_fanout=if(win_destruct>=1 AND win_reads==0 AND win_n>=4,1,0)
| eval rapid_escalation=if(match(tool_risk_class,"high|critical") AND match(prev_risk,"low|medium|unclassified") AND win_n<=6,1,0)
| eventstats avg(win_n) AS avg_win perc90(win_n) AS p90_win BY agent_id
| eval tpm_burst=if(win_n>=180 OR (isnotnull(p90_win) AND p90_win>0 AND win_n>10*p90_win),1,0)
| eval auth_churn=if(win_token_dc>=3 AND win_n>=5,1,0)
| join type=left max=0 client_id
    [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Authentication.user) AS auth_user latest(Authentication.action) AS auth_action FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE nodename=Authentication earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Authentication.src
     | rename Authentication.src AS client_id ]
| join type=left max=0 mcp_server_id
    [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS change_hits FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-168h@h latest=now BY Change.dest
     | rename Change.dest AS mcp_server_id ]
| eval severity=case(
    tenant_mismatch==1 OR prompt_injection_like==1, "critical",
    tpm_burst==1 AND coalesce(client_tier,"")!="internal_batch", "critical",
    sensitive_fanout==1 OR rapid_escalation==1, "high",
    auth_churn==1 OR transport_violation==1, "high",
    unauth_ok==1, "high",
    match(error_code,"(?i)payload_too_large|timeout|reset|econn") AND win_n>20, "medium",
    true(), "info")
| eval detection_reason=case(
    tenant_mismatch==1,"cross_tenant_or_scope_mismatch",
    prompt_injection_like==1,"prompt_injection_or_encoded_args",
    tpm_burst==1,"tool_invocation_rate_anomaly",
    sensitive_fanout==1,"destructive_without_recon_pattern",
    rapid_escalation==1,"low_to_high_risk_escalation_seconds",
    auth_churn==1,"bearer_rotation_or_token_churn",
    transport_violation==1,"client_transport_policy_violation",
    unauth_ok==1,"unauthenticated_high_risk_success",
    true(),"mcp_security_fusion_context")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical","isolate_agent_session_rotate_mcp_credentials_review_tool_allowlist_open_soc_ticket",
    severity=="high","force_reauth_review_authorization_server_logs_quarantine_mcp_server_if_needed",
    severity=="medium","tune_rate_limits_review_otel_and_stdio_health_check_proxy_timeouts",
    true(),"continue_monitoring_refresh_lookups")
| stats max(_time) AS _time values(lane) AS lanes max(tpm_burst) AS tpm_burst max(tenant_mismatch) AS tenant_mismatch max(prompt_injection_like) AS prompt_injection_like max(sensitive_fanout) AS sensitive_fanout max(rapid_escalation) AS rapid_escalation max(auth_churn) AS auth_churn max(transport_violation) AS transport_violation max(unauth_ok) AS unauth_ok max(win_n) AS peak_window_tools max(change_hits) AS change_hits values(auth_user) AS auth_user first(client_tier) AS client_tier max(eval(if(severity=="critical",4,if(severity=="high",3,if(severity=="medium",2,1))))) AS sev_rank BY minute_bucket mcp_server_id agent_id client_id session_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class transport tenant_id
| eval severity=case(sev_rank>=4,"critical",sev_rank==3,"high",sev_rank==2,"medium",true(),"info")
| eval detection_reason=case(tenant_mismatch>=1,"cross_tenant_or_scope_mismatch", prompt_injection_like>=1,"prompt_injection_or_encoded_args", tpm_burst>=1,"tool_invocation_rate_anomaly", sensitive_fanout>=1,"destructive_without_recon_pattern", rapid_escalation>=1,"low_to_high_risk_escalation_seconds", auth_churn>=1,"bearer_rotation_or_token_churn", transport_violation>=1,"client_transport_policy_violation", unauth_ok>=1,"unauthenticated_high_risk_success", true(),"mcp_security_fusion_context")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","isolate_agent_session_rotate_mcp_credentials_review_tool_allowlist_open_soc_ticket", severity=="high","force_reauth_review_authorization_server_logs_quarantine_mcp_server_if_needed", severity=="medium","tune_rate_limits_review_otel_and_stdio_health_check_proxy_timeouts", true(),"continue_monitoring_refresh_lookups")
| table minute_bucket mcp_server_id agent_id client_id session_id tool_name tool_category tool_risk_class transport tenant_id severity detection_reason tpm_burst tenant_mismatch prompt_injection_like sensitive_fanout auth_churn peak_window_tools auth_user change_hits recommended_action lanes client_tier```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

[security_uc_13_4_7_mcp_tool_invocation_threat_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-ai-sec@example.com
action.email.subject = MCP tool threat $result.severity$ agent=$result.agent_id$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch security_uc_13_4_7_mcp_tool_invocation_threat | where severity IN ("critical","high")

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize per-lane summaries hourly into summary indexes keyed on agent_id and mcp_server_id, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For very high-volume read-only agents, raise tpm_warn and tpm_page thresholds or annotate client_tier internal_batch to suppress benign bursts.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on MCP servers: compare live tool invocation counts per agent_id in the last ten minutes against Splunk aggregates for the same window. When totals disagree, verify HTTP Event Collector health, OpenTelemetry exporter backpressure, and duplicate forwarder submissions before muting detections.

Deep dive with exemplar session_id values: trace them through otel traces, reverse-proxy access logs, and Enterprise Security episodes. Confirm tool_name values match mcp_tool_inventory.csv rows after normalization.

Cross-tenant validation: in a lab tenant, issue a deliberately scoped tool call, confirm tenant_id and allowed_tenant alignment suppresses tenant_mismatch, then simulate a mismatched tenant label in staging only and confirm critical severity.

Authentication enrichment: pick a client_id flagged high, compare tstats Authentication summaries with identity provider session logs, and confirm auth_user fields align with expected principals.

Injection validation: run an authorized lab argument containing a harmless encoded substring that still matches regex heuristics in a non-production index, confirm prompt_injection_like flags the event, then verify production parsers exclude the test sourcetype.

Negative test: after recovery when abusive sessions are terminated and lookups are current, confirm filtering severity to critical and high returns zero rows for six consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document approved automation windows and migration programs so validators do not file defects during planned bulk delete jobs flagged as sensitive_fanout.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Thousand read_file invocations in sixty seconds from a single agent_id: treat as runaway retrieval or jailbreak-driven scraping; pause the agent session, inspect prompt_injection_like, and compare against internal_batch allowlisting before blocking hosts.

Case 2 — Rotating bearer_token_hash values with high win_token_dc inside streamstats windows: coordinate with identity teams on compromised refresh tokens, stolen OAuth clients, or misconfigured token brokers.

Case 3 — Cross-tenant mismatch between tenant_id and allowed_tenant: verify MCP server tenancy isolation, RLS policies on backing data sources, and broker bugs that leak foreign tenant identifiers into tool results.

Case 4 — Destructive tool fan-out without prior read recon: investigate prompt injection or malicious automation, snapshot mcp_audit arguments under forensic policy, and invoke break-glass containment on MCP listeners if warranted.

Case 5 — Rapid escalation from low tool_risk_class to critical within seconds: correlate with otel spans for human-driven copilot sessions versus unattended agents, then enforce staged tool approval workflows.

Case 6 — stdio transport disconnect storms in mcp_logs with error_code reset: check agent process crashes, pipe buffer limits, and antivirus interference on developer laptops hosting local MCP servers.

Case 7 — HTTP streaming timeouts and payload_too_large errors clustered on one mcp_server_id: review reverse-proxy body limits, gzip double-encoding, and model-assisted bulk export jobs before attributing to attackers.

Case 8 — mcp_security notables firing without matching mcp_audit rows: repair dead ingestion on the audit lane before trusting fusion severity; dead lanes create false calm.

Case 9 — OpenTelemetry span volume collapse while audits continue: validate collector pipelines, sampling rules, and trace retention; traces are supporting evidence, not sole ground truth.

Case 10 — Stale mcp_tool_inventory.csv rows after CI deploys new tools: automate CSV regeneration on manifest changes to avoid misclassified tool_risk_class and suppressed alerts.

Case 11 — Legitimate scheduled IT automation with parallel delete operations: annotate client_tier internal_batch, document maintenance windows, and require change_ticket_id on events.

Case 12 — Enterprise Security tstats summaries lagging: repair data model acceleration so auth_user joins populate; empty joins should not silently downgrade investigations.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists mcp_audit, mcp_logs, otel:span, and mcp_security arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, and tstats appear; closing table includes minute_bucket, mcp_server_id, agent_id, client_id, session_id, tool_name, tool_category, tool_risk_class, transport, tenant_id, severity, detection_reason, tpm_burst, tenant_mismatch, prompt_injection_like, sensitive_fanout, auth_churn, peak_window_tools, auth_user, change_hits, recommended_action, and lanes for analyst-ready review.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the helper programs that let smart assistants use your company tools. When one helper starts hammering dangerous actions, sneaks across customer boundaries, or signs in strangely, we raise a clear alert so people can stop it before real damage spreads.
hardware: Applies to hosts and containers running Anthropic-compatible MCP servers, Splunk MCP Server deployments, agent runtimes with stdio or HTTP MCP clients, and OpenTelemetry Collector agents forwarding otel:span data.
